what_it_does: Stores an interpretive JSON reading of a Markdown template that analysts fill in when auditing an ingested source folder of design pages, cataloguing the template's sections, measurement discipline, evidence rules, and downstream gap-routing conventions.
observations: The file is an interpretation of `_TEMPLATE.md`, not the template source itself.

It describes the template as a blank checklist with sections for Frame, Grid & spatial structure, Tonal zoning, Type, Colour & brand, Atoms, Layout patterns, Sequence & rhythm, Construction, Responsive rules, Diff vs current system, and Gaps to log.
It records that the template header carries provenance placeholders for source path, page count, copy location, and status checkboxes.
It notes the percentage-of-frame convention for all spatial and typographic measurements.
It notes the instruction to sample real pixels and record hex values plus locations for tonal zoning and colour.
It captures a diff legend (✅ confirms, ➕ new, ✏️ generalises, ⚠️ contradicts) and a routing instruction to copy gaps to SYSTEM-GAPS.md.
It lists concerns about ambiguous mutually-unchecked status boxes and assumed sibling-file existence.
It closes with embedding-oriented summary and intention sentences.
standouts: Every spatial and type measurement is encoded as a percentage of the frame, enforcing a resolution-independent reading discipline.
The template mandates sampling real pixels (hex + coordinates) rather than relying on impressionistic descriptions.
It includes a built-in diff-vs-current-system legend and an extract-and-route rule for logging gaps.
conventions: JSON interpretive note keyed by project, path, and model.
Provenance-first metadata (source path, page count, copy location, status).
Markdown template with placeholder tokens such as <folder>, #______, and __%.
concerns: The status header uses two unchecked boxes for 'in progress' and 'complete' with no enforced single-state selection.
It references sibling files (SYSTEM-GAPS.md, _ingest/<folder>/) whose existence and upkeep are assumed but not guaranteed from this file alone.
Because the subject is a template, many claims describe intended analyst behavior rather than observed concrete behavior.
notes: An interpretive JSON reading of a design-deck audit template that enforces a percentage-of-frame, pixel-sampled, diff-against-system protocol.
questions: Is SYSTEM-GAPS.md the live gap register, and who reconciles entries copied into it?
What distinguishes ingest/<folder>/ from _ingest/<folder>/ in the provenance header?
Who fills the template and how are analyses validated for consistency across folders?
How are percentage-of-frame measurements derived when source media have varying aspect ratios?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file is an interpretive analysis of a template and it documents exactly the structured checklist and conventions the template is meant to enforce.
apparent_themes: axis=f(content,position) (frame → grid → zoning → type → atoms → patterns → responsive)
intention_signals: Inferred: to standardize how raw design decks are read into a comparable design-DNA by forcing every analyst to capture the same frame/grid/zoning/type/atom/pattern dimensions in the same units and to classify each finding against the existing system.
novelty: Unusual in treating a design audit as a measured, percentage-of-frame, pixel-sampled protocol with a built-in confirms/new/generalises/contradicts diff legend — closer to a measurement instrument than a notes template.
contribution: Contributes a structured interpretive record of the audit schema/protocol, capturing the template's percentage-based measurement discipline, real-pixel sampling rule, layout/sequence/responsive categories, diff legend, and gap-routing convention — a reusable lens for turning raw decks into comparable findings.
summary_for_embedding: JSON interpretive analysis of a percentage-of-frame, pixel-sampled design-deck audit template with sections for frame, grid, tonal zoning, type, colour, atoms, patterns, sequence, construction, responsive rules, diff legend, and gap logging.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to document the standardized protocol for reading source design decks into comparable design-DNA and routing gaps back to the system register.
